acceptance_criteria: Prove list-compilation composition for compileStmts by structural induction.
Strengthen append theorem so it can be instantiated from any initial CompileState.
Add a non-trivial semantic-preservation theorem artifact for a supported compilation subset without weakening obligations.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to the supported letVar/assignVar/setStorage(localVar) subset with explicit field-resolution assumptions.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to an arithmetic subset where assignVar rebinding uses add(localVar,tmpLiteral) before setStorage.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to an arithmetic subset where assignVar rebinding uses sub(localVar,tmpLiteral) before setStorage.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to an arithmetic subset where assignVar rebinding uses mul(localVar,tmpLiteral) before setStorage.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a return subset where `return (literal n)` preserves source halting semantics.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a let-then-return subset where `letVar tmp (literal n); return (localVar tmp)` preserves source variable-binding and halting semantics.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a branch subset where `ite (eq (literal n) (literal m))` selects between two `setStorage` literal writes under explicit field-resolution assumptions.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a require subset where `require (eq (literal n) (literal m)) message` preserves source guard/revert behavior.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a require subset where `require (lt (literal n) (literal m)) message` preserves source guard/revert behavior.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a require subset where `require (gt (literal n) (literal m)) message` preserves source guard/revert behavior.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a require subset where `require (ge (literal n) (literal m)) message` preserves source guard/revert behavior.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a require subset where `require (le (literal n) (literal m)) message` preserves source guard/revert behavior.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a require subset where `require (logicalNot (eq (literal n) (literal m))) message` preserves source guard/revert behavior.

Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a composite-guard require subset where `require (logicalAnd (eq (literal n) (literal m)) (lt (literal p) (literal q))) message` preserves source short-circuit guard/revert behavior.

Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a composite-guard require subset where `require (logicalOr (eq (literal n) (literal m)) (lt (literal p) (literal q))) message` preserves source short-circuit guard/revert behavior.
Generalize single-guard require semantic preservation into one theorem over a guard family (`eq`, `notEq`, `lt`, `gt`, `ge`, `le`) without deleting existing specialized obligations.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to lists of supported single-guard `require` literals via structural induction, preserving short-circuit revert behavior.

Generalize require semantic preservation into one theorem over a unified guard family combining binary guards (`eq`, `notEq`, `lt`, `gt`, `ge`, `le`) with composite guards (`logicalAnd(eq,lt)`, `logicalOr(eq,lt)`) without deleting existing obligations.

Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a sequencing subset where a unified require guard-family clause executes before `setStorage fieldName (literal writeVal)`, preserving short-circuit revert behavior and subsequent storage update under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a sequencing subset where a unified require guard-family clause executes before `return (literal retVal)`, preserving short-circuit revert vs halting semantics.

Extend semantic-preservation coverage to lists of unified require guard-family clauses (`binary`, `logicalAnd(eq,lt)`, `logicalOr(eq,lt)`) via structural induction, preserving short-circuit revert behavior.

Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a sequencing subset where a unified require guard-family clause list executes before `setStorage fieldName (literal writeVal)`, preserving clause-list short-circuit revert behavior and subsequent storage update under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a sequencing subset where a unified require guard-family clause list executes before `return (literal retVal)`, preserving clause-list short-circuit revert vs halting semantics.

Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a sequencing subset where a unified require guard-family clause list executes before `letVar tmp (literal retVal); return (localVar tmp)`, preserving clause-list short-circuit revert behavior together with local-binding return semantics.

Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a sequencing subset where a unified require guard-family clause list executes before `letVar tmp (literal n); assignVar tmp (add (localVar tmp) (literal m)); setStorage fieldName (localVar tmp)`, preserving clause-list short-circuit revert behavior together with arithmetic rebinding/storage semantics under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a sequencing subset where a unified require guard-family clause list executes before `letVar tmp (literal n); assignVar tmp (sub (localVar tmp) (literal m)); setStorage fieldName (localVar tmp)`, preserving clause-list short-circuit revert behavior together with arithmetic rebinding/storage semantics under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a sequencing subset where a unified require guard-family clause list executes before `letVar tmp (literal n); assignVar tmp (mul (localVar tmp) (literal m)); setStorage fieldName (localVar tmp)`, preserving clause-list short-circuit revert behavior together with arithmetic rebinding/storage semantics under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

Introduce one generic dispatcher theorem over supported require-clause-list sequencing tails (`setStorage literal`, `return literal`, `let-return local`, `let-assign-{add,sub,mul}-setStorage local`) to reduce pattern-by-pattern theorem surface into a single reusable 2.2 artifact without deleting existing obligations.

theorems.name: compileStmts_append
theorems.statement_summary: Compiling pre ++ post is equivalent to sequentially compiling pre then post.

theorems.name: compileStmts_append_from
theorems.statement_summary: The append-composition theorem holds when run from any initial CompileState.

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_setStorage_literal_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for setStorage literal emits the expected typed setStorage write under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

theorems.name: compile_setStorage_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported setStorage-literal subset, compiled typed-IR execution matches direct source-level storage update semantics.

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_literal_setStorage_local_run
theorems.statement_summary: Two-statement compilation for letVar-literal followed by setStorage-localVar emits the expected SSA let and typed setStorage sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported letVar/setStorage-local subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source storage-update semantics and records the expected SSA variable binding effect.

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_assign_literal_setStorage_local_run
theorems.statement_summary: Three-statement compilation for letVar-literal; assignVar-literal; setStorage-localVar emits the expected two SSA lets and typed setStorage sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_assign_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported letVar/assignVar/setStorage-local subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source storage-update semantics and records the expected SSA rebinding sequence.

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_assign_add_literal_setStorage_local_run
theorems.statement_summary: Three-statement compilation for letVar-literal; assignVar-add(localVar,literal); setStorage-localVar emits the expected SSA let rebinding with typed addition and typed setStorage sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_assign_add_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported arithmetic letVar/assignVar-add/setStorage-local subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source storage-update semantics (n + m) and the expected SSA rebinding effect.

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_assign_sub_literal_setStorage_local_run
theorems.statement_summary: Three-statement compilation for letVar-literal; assignVar-sub(localVar,literal); setStorage-localVar emits the expected SSA let rebinding with typed subtraction and typed setStorage sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_assign_sub_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported arithmetic letVar/assignVar-sub/setStorage-local subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source storage-update semantics (n - m) and the expected SSA rebinding effect.

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_assign_mul_literal_setStorage_local_run
theorems.statement_summary: Three-statement compilation for letVar-literal; assignVar-mul(localVar,literal); setStorage-localVar emits the expected SSA let rebinding with typed multiplication and typed setStorage sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_assign_mul_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported arithmetic letVar/assignVar-mul/setStorage-local subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source storage-update semantics (n * m) and the expected SSA rebinding effect.

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_return_literal_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for return literal emits the expected typed returnUint statement from any initial compile state.

theorems.name: compile_return_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported return-literal subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source return semantics (state unchanged, halting).

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_return_local_literal_run
theorems.statement_summary: Two-statement compilation for letVar-literal; return-localVar emits the expected SSA let and typed returnUint sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_return_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported let-then-return subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source variable-binding and halting semantics.

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_ite_eq_setStorage_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for ite(eq(literal,literal)) with setStorage literal branches emits the expected typed if_ with typed setStorage branches from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_ite_eq_setStorage_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported branch subset with an equality guard over literals, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source branch-selected storage update semantics.

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_eq_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(eq(literal,literal), message) emits the expected typed if_ with a revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_eq_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported require-eq-literals subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source require semantics (state unchanged on true, revert on false).

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_not_eq_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(logicalNot(eq(literal,literal)), message) emits the expected typed if_ with a typed not(eq(...)) guard and revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_not_eq_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported require-not-eq-literals subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source require semantics (state unchanged when n != m, revert otherwise).

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_lt_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(lt(literal,literal), message) emits the expected typed if_ with a revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_lt_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported require-lt-literals subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source require semantics (state unchanged when n < m, revert otherwise).

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_gt_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(gt(literal,literal), message) emits the expected typed if_ with guard lt(rhs,lhs) and a revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_gt_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported require-gt-literals subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source require semantics (state unchanged when n > m, revert otherwise).

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_ge_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(ge(literal,literal), message) emits the expected typed if_ with guard not(lt(...)) and a revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_ge_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported require-ge-literals subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source require semantics (state unchanged when n >= m, revert otherwise).

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_le_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(le(literal,literal), message) emits the expected typed if_ with guard not(lt(rhs,lhs)) and a revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_le_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported require-le-literals subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source require semantics (state unchanged when n <= m, revert otherwise).

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_and_eq_lt_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(logicalAnd(eq(literal,literal), lt(literal,literal)), message) emits the expected typed if_ with a typed and-guard and revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_and_eq_lt_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported composite require guard subset (eq && lt over literals), compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source short-circuit require semantics.

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_or_eq_lt_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(logicalOr(eq(literal,literal), lt(literal,literal)), message) emits the expected typed if_ with a typed or-guard and revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_or_eq_lt_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported composite require guard subset (eq || lt over literals), compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source short-circuit require semantics.

theorems.name: compile_require_binary_literal_guard_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Generalized theorem: for guard family `eq/notEq/lt/gt/ge/le`, compiled single-guard require execution equals source require semantics.

theorems.name: compile_require_binary_literal_clauses_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Structural-induction theorem: for lists of supported single-guard require clauses, compiled evaluation equals source evaluation with identical short-circuit revert behavior.

theorems.name: compile_require_literal_guard_family_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Generalized theorem: for unified require guard families (binary guard set plus and(eq,lt)/or(eq,lt)), compiled single-require execution equals source require semantics.

theorems.name: compile_require_family_then_setStorage_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Sequencing theorem: for unified require guard families followed by setStorage-literal, compiled execution matches source short-circuit require semantics plus storage update under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

theorems.name: compile_require_family_then_return_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Sequencing theorem: for unified require guard families followed by return-literal, compiled execution matches source short-circuit require semantics and halting behavior.

theorems.name: compile_require_literal_guard_family_clauses_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Structural-induction theorem: for lists of unified require guard-family clauses (`binary`, `andEqLt`, `orEqLt`), compiled evaluation equals source evaluation with identical short-circuit revert behavior.

theorems.name: compile_require_family_clauses_then_setStorage_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Sequencing theorem: for unified require guard-family clause lists followed by setStorage-literal, compiled execution matches source clause-list short-circuit semantics plus storage update under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

theorems.name: compile_require_family_clauses_then_return_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Sequencing theorem: for unified require guard-family clause lists followed by return-literal, compiled execution matches source clause-list short-circuit semantics and halting behavior.

theorems.name: compile_require_family_clauses_then_let_return_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Sequencing theorem: for unified require guard-family clause lists followed by letVar-literal then return-localVar, compiled execution matches source clause-list short-circuit semantics together with local-binding return behavior.

theorems.name: compile_require_family_clauses_then_let_assign_add_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Sequencing theorem: for unified require guard-family clause lists followed by letVar/assignVar-add/setStorage-localVar, compiled execution matches source clause-list short-circuit semantics together with arithmetic rebinding/storage behavior under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

theorems.name: compile_require_family_clauses_then_let_assign_sub_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Sequencing theorem: for unified require guard-family clause lists followed by letVar/assignVar-sub/setStorage-localVar, compiled execution matches source clause-list short-circuit semantics together with arithmetic rebinding/storage behavior under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

theorems.name: compile_require_family_clauses_then_let_assign_mul_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Sequencing theorem: for unified require guard-family clause lists followed by letVar/assignVar-mul/setStorage-localVar, compiled execution matches source clause-list short-circuit semantics together with arithmetic rebinding/storage behavior under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

theorems.name: compile_require_family_clauses_then_tail_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: Generic sequencing semantic-preservation theorem over supported tails after unified require guard-family clause lists, proved by tail-family case split reusing existing specialized theorems.

theorems.name: execCompiledRequireFamilyClausesThenTail
theorems.statement_summary: Compiled semantics dispatcher over the supported tail family after unified require guard-family clause lists.

theorems.name: execSourceRequireFamilyClausesThenTail
theorems.statement_summary: Source semantics dispatcher over the supported tail family after unified require guard-family clause lists.

2.2.notes: 2.2 remains partial: this run introduces `compile_require_family_clauses_then_tail_semantics` as one generic theorem spanning the currently supported require-clause-list continuation family without deleting prior obligations. Uncovered fragment remains full statement-fragment-wide source↔typed theorem over arbitrary supported statement lists.
uncovered_fragments: No single theorem yet states source-semantics ↔ typed-semantics preservation for arbitrary lists of the declared supported statement fragment.
Current genericization is over require-clause-list continuation tails only; broader statement constructors still require integration into one fragment-wide theorem.
